message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Workshop Kitchen + Bar (Palm Springs)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 1,596 reviews; Google 1,250+ reviews; OpenTable 1,788 ratings; Tripadvisor 831 reviews — aggregate 5,000+ across platforms
- Sentiment: mostly positive with notable mixed signal (Yelp/Tripadvisor reviews split between exceptional and disappointed; Google 4.3 and OpenTable 4.4 anchor positive)
- Longevity: Operating since October 2012 (13+ years); original chef/owner Michael Beckman and co-owner Joseph Mourani still attached per current site — ownership stable
- Credentials: ["James Beard Foundation Award — Outstanding Restaurant Design, 75+ seats (2015, awarded to SOMA Architects for the space)","Michelin Guide recommended — multi-year (described as 4-time Michelin recommended; Michelin Plate 2024 and 2025)","Restaurant & Bar Design Awards — Best Design for the Americas (2013)","Featured by Palm Springs Life, Hotels Above Par, Time Out Palm Springs and regional best-of fine-dining lists"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces reliably for 'Michelin restaurants Palm Springs', 'James Beard Palm Springs', 'fine dining Palm Springs', and 'best restaurants Palm Springs uptown design district'. Less reliably surfaced in broader 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' agent rollups, which tend to lead with Le Vallauris, The Steakhouse (Agua Caliente), Edge Steakhouse, Copley's, and Wally's. Does not surface for cuisine-specific queries like 'New American near Palm Springs' as consistently as its reputation would suggest — agents often default to Eight4Nine, Trio, and Jake's for that bracket.
- Site structured data: Weak. workshopkitchenbar.com presents address, hours, and menu in human-readable text only. No schema.org JSON-LD Restaurant/LocalBusiness markup detected, no clear Open Graph or microdata. Hours wording ('Sunday Brunch Everyday 10am-2pm, 5pm-10pm') is ambiguous for parsers. Resy handles reservations off-site. Strong human design, weak agent-readable signal.
- Visibility gap score: 6
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Workshop Kitchen Bar and its Michelin recognition:

## Michelin Guide Recognition

Workshop Kitchen & Bar is a restaurant in the 2025 MICHELIN Guide USA. More specifically, it is a 4-time Michelin recommended & James Beard awarded restaurant in Palm Springs.

## Restaurant Details

The restaurant is located at 800 N. Palm Canyon Dr., Ste. G, Palm Springs, CA, 92262, USA and serves Californian cuisine with a $$$ price range.

## Culinary Philosophy

The restaurant features farm-to-table food utilizing the Coachella Valley's productive agricultural region. A dedicated garden in Rancho Mirage, along with a sizeable herb planter behind the kitchen, furnishes peak-season produce for wood-fired dishes like a flat iron au poivre, and even a simple yuzu tart is prettied up with farm-fresh raspberries and wood sorrel.

## Design and Atmosphere

The setting is a Spanish-style building from the 1920s, while the interior is a modernist temple of polished concrete—a mist-cooled courtyard provides additional seating.

## Bar Program

Special touches like house-made ginger beer distinguish an exemplary bar program, with cocktails both classic and creative.

Based on the search results, here's comprehensive information about Workshop Kitchen Bar in Palm Springs and its James Beard Award-winning design:

## Award and Recognition

Workshop Kitchen + Bar won Palm Springs' first James Beard award for Outstanding Restaurant Design in the category of over 75 seats. The award was presented in 2015 for best Restaurant Design or Renovation in North America.

## Design Details

The restaurant's design was created by Michel Abboud, principal and founder of SOMA Architects. The design approach used a simple insertion of poured in place cast concrete brutalist elements with an ecclesiastic design approach.

The interior features a 27-foot cathedral ceiling and soaring 17-foot monolithic concrete sleek booths. In the center aisle, a 35-foot long communal table acts as a more public space, while large monolithic concrete booths provide a more intimate dining experience.

## Location and Context

The restaurant is located in the historic El Paseo Building on North Palm Canyon. Because the restaurant is housed in a Palm Springs Class One Historic Building, no external revisions could be made, so the design impact was created entirely in the interior.

## Chef and Ownership

The restaurant is helmed by Chef/Owner Michael Beckman, along with partner Joseph Mourani.

Here are some of the best fine dining restaurants in the Coachella Valley:

## French & European Cuisine

Le Vallauris in Palm Springs is a top choice for classic French cuisine in a romantic setting, while Chez Pierre Bistro in Palm Desert offers authentic French fine dining with an eclectic menu and extensive wine list.

## Steakhouses & Seafood

Morton's The Steakhouse in Palm Desert specializes in prime-aged beef and offers a classic steakhouse experience with an upscale atmosphere and impeccable service. Eddie V's Prime Seafood is a luxury dining destination known for seafood flown in from pristine waters around the world, with artistically prepared dishes and critically acclaimed prime center-cut steaks.

## Unique Dining Experiences

The Purple Room Supper Club in Palm Springs offers a unique blend of fine dining and live entertainment, known for its sophisticated ambiance and classic American cuisine. Copley's On Palm Canyon is housed in a historic property that was once Cary Grant's estate and offers a mix of American and European cuisine with seasonal ingredients, featuring al fresco and indoor dining.

## Farm-to-Table & International

Morgan's in the Desert at La Quinta Resort & Club offers refined dining with farm-to-table cuisine inspired by flavors of the American Southwest, using local ingredients. Trilussa in Cathedral City is known for its classic Italian cuisine, offering traditional Italian dishes prepared with authentic flavors.

From sophisticated steakhouses to intimate lounges, the Valley offers a wide range of fine dining experiences.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best fine dining restaurants in Palm Springs for 2026:

## Top Fine Dining Establishments

Workshop Kitchen & Bar holds both a James Beard Foundation design award and Michelin recommendation, making it the city's most decorated fine dining venue. Located at 800 N Palm Canyon Drive, it's known for its farm-to-table approach and industrial-chic design.

Rooster and the Pig was named USA Today's Restaurant of the Year in 2026, confirming Palm Springs' national culinary standing. Located at 356 S Indian Canyon Drive, it serves Vietnamese-Californian cuisine with signature dishes including shaking beef and spicy chile okra.

4 Saints, the rooftop dining room at the Kimpton Rowan Palm Springs, is one of the restaurants that made the MICHELIN Guide, with indoor and outdoor seating and wide-open views.

## Additional Notable Options

Bar Issi offers a coastal Italian menu with wood-fired pizzas and housemade pastas, and its strong cocktail program and polished dining room explain why reservations can be hard to come by.

Maleza is one of the best places to eat in Palm Springs, where Chef Ysaac Ramirez's bright, flavor-driven dishes draw on Mexico City influences.

Le Vallauris is a cornerstone of fine dining in Palm Springs, offering an elegant French cuisine experience set in a historic home with a beautiful garden patio, featuring classic French dishes and impeccable service.

Both Workshop Kitchen & Bar and Rooster and the Pig require advance reservations during peak season, November through April.

**Awards & Recognition:**

Web search results for query: "Workshop Kitchen Bar Palm Springs opened 2012 Michael Beckman"

Links: [{"title":"Eclectic Kim Review: Workshop Kitchen & Bar","url":"https://www.eclectickim.com/workshop-kitchen-bar/"},{"title":"Workshop Kitchen | Michelin Recommended Restaurant Palm Springs","url":"https://www.workshopkitchenbar.com/"},{"title":"Palm Springs | Workshop Kitchen+Bar","url":"https://www.workshopkitchenbar.com/?p=rose"},{"title":"Michael Beckman's Workshop Kitchen & Bar Is Now Open in Los Angeles","url":"https://robbreport.com/food-drink/dining/workshop-kitchen-bar-los-angeles-1234738815/"},{"title":"Workshop Kitchen + Bar - goop","url":"https://goop.com/place/california/palm-springs/movie-colony-restaurants/workshop-kitchen/"},{"title":"Workshop Kitchen | Palm Springs Catering & Events","url":"https://www.workshopkitchencatering.com/"},{"title":"WORKSHOP KITCHEN & BAR - Updated May 2026 - 1507 Photos & 1596 Reviews - 800 N Palm Canyon Dr, Palm Springs, California - New American - Restaurant Reviews - Phone Number - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/biz/workshop-kitchen-and-bar-palm-springs-2"},{"title":"Workshop Kitchen & Bar","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/listing/workshop-kitchen-%26-bar/11/"},{"title":"Workshop Kitchen & Bar in Palm Springs | Michelin-Recommended Dining","url":"https://www.hotelsabovepar.com/articles/restaurants/workshop-kitchen-bar-palm-springs-michelin-restaurant"},{"title":"Workshop for a Whole New World – Modern, Architecture, Design, Experiences","url":"https://mwkly.com/places/workshop-for-a-whole-new-world/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Workshop Kitchen Bar in Palm Springs:

Workshop Kitchen and Bar opened on September 22, 2012. Chef Michael Beckman and Chef Joe Mourani met while studying at the Paul Bocuse Institute in Lyon, France, and in 2012, they partnered together to repurpose the 1926 historic El Paseo building in the Palm Springs Design District.

The chefs then partnered with New York-based architect and artist Michel Abboud, and together they converted the space into a temple of modernist concrete. In 2013 the London-based Restaurant & Bar Design Awards named Workshop "Best Design for the Americas" followed by an "Outstanding Restaurant Design, 76 Seats & Over" win in 2015 by the James Beard Foundation.

The restaurant features farm-to-table cuisine with locally sourced ingredients, serving sustainable, elevated California cuisine from their chef's garden.

Here's what I found about Workshop Kitchen in Palm Springs:

## Overview
Workshop Kitchen & Bar is a 4-time Michelin recommended & James Beard awarded restaurant in Palm Springs. It's a New American restaurant located at 800 N Palm Canyon Dr, Ste G, Palm Springs, CA.

## Cuisine & Concept
The restaurant features farm-to-table cuisine with locally sourced ingredients. It's known for its farm-to-table New American fare that features everything from goat cheese pierogi and Doro Wat chicken to ribeye, pork chops, and English Pea risotto.

## Location & Design
The restaurant is located in a 1926 historic El Paseo building in the Palm Springs Design District. The interior is a modernist temple of polished concrete—a mist-cooled courtyard provides additional seating.

## Hours & Contact
The restaurant is open daily 5-10pm, with Sunday brunch. You can reach them at (760) 459-3451 or visit workshopkitchenbar.com.

## Leadership
Chef Michael Beckman and Chef Joe Mourani met while studying at the Paul Bocuse Institute in Lyon, France. In 2012, they partnered together to repurpose the 1926 historic El Paseo building.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best romantic restaurants in Palm Springs for a date night:

## Top Romantic Restaurants

**Mister Parker's**: Known as the most romantic restaurant in Palm Springs, it features an upscale fine dining destination with an impressive wine list and cocktails. Jackets and collared shirts are required for gentlemen, as it's a return to an era when people dressed to impress.

**Le Vallauris Restaurant**: A refined dining destination offering a prix-fixe French-focused menu and romantic patio setting, housed in a 1927 Mediterranean revival home and a local favorite for over four decades. The outdoor setting under century-old banyan trees, paired with warm ambient lighting, creates an enchanting atmosphere.

**Melvyn's**: Located on the premises of the legendary Ingleside Estate hotel, Melvyn's is elegantly upbeat and the home of authentic Hollywood glamour, offering martinis, live music, and sophisticated night out options.

**FARM**: A traditional Provencial-style restaurant in downtown Palm Springs with a menu of traditional European dishes made with locally sourced produce, offering prix fixe dinners in an outdoor setting that transports you to the South of France.

**Il Corso**: An authentic Italian dining restaurant with 'beautiful ambiance' and 'excellent food and service,' with 'lovely atmosphere' and 'impeccable service' making it perfect for a date night.

These restaurants offer a range of cuisines and atmospheres, from French fine dining to Italian classics, all well-suited for romantic evenings in Palm Springs.

content.text: I have enough verification. Let me synthesize.

confirms: Yelp review count of 1,596 confirmed verbatim on Yelp listing
James Beard Foundation Award 2015 for Outstanding Restaurant Design (76 Seats & Over) — confirmed via PR Newswire and Palm Springs Life; awarded to SOMA Architects/Michel Abboud for the space
Restaurant & Bar Design Awards 2013 'Best Design for the Americas' — confirmed via multiple sources and the restaurant's own About page
Michelin Guide recommended (listed in 2025 USA Michelin Guide; described as '4-time Michelin recommended' in multiple sources)
Ownership stable — Michael Beckman (Chef/Owner) and Joseph Mourani (co-owner) still attached per current website About page; they met at Paul Bocuse Institute and partnered in 2012
Featured by Palm Springs Life, Hotels Above Par, Time Out Palm Springs, AFAR, and regional best-of fine-dining lists — confirmed
Surfaces reliably for 'Michelin restaurants Palm Springs' and 'James Beard Palm Springs' queries (top-of-results in both)

Site structured data assessment confirmed — no JSON-LD schema.org Restaurant/LocalBusiness markup, no Open Graph, no microdata on workshopkitchenbar.com; hours and address are human-readable text only

Broader 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' rollups tend to lead with Le Vallauris, Morton's, Eddie V's, Copley's, Chez Pierre, Morgan's in the Desert — Workshop is NOT prominently surfaced in valley-wide fine dining rollups, consistent with assessment
Google rating 4.3 and OpenTable rating 4.4 anchors confirmed via aggregated review data
contradicts: Claim states 'Operating since October 2012' — multiple sources (including operator history) state Workshop Kitchen + Bar opened September 22, 2012, not October. Minor but factual error.

Claim states website hours wording 'Sunday Brunch Everyday 10am-2pm, 5pm-10pm' — the actual current site wording per fetch is 'Sunday Brunch: 10am-2pm daily' and 'Dinner: 5pm-10pm everyday' (two separate lines). The garbled phrasing in the claim appears to misrepresent the site copy, though both versions are still ambiguous for parsers so the underlying weak-signal conclusion stands.
flagged_as_unverified: Google 1,250+ reviews — could not independently confirm exact count via search results
OpenTable 1,788 ratings — exact count not surfaced in search results (OpenTable listing exists but specific count not visible)
Tripadvisor 831 reviews — exact count not independently confirmed in search results
Aggregate 5,000+ across platforms — depends on unverified individual platform counts
Sentiment characterization 'split between exceptional and disappointed' on Yelp/Tripadvisor — directional plausibility but not adversarially sampled review-by-review
Specific assertion that 'agents often default to Eight4Nine, Trio, and Jake's' for New American near Palm Springs queries — not directly tested in this run
Visibility gap score of 6 and 'convertible' tier — analyst-derived judgments, not externally verifiable facts
Whether Michelin Plate designation specifically applied in both 2024 and 2025 (Michelin Guide listing confirmed but plate-vs-recommended-by-year not granularly verified)
input.notes: Core credentials (James Beard 2015, R&B Design Awards 2013, Michelin recommendation, ownership continuity, weak structured-data signal) all hold up cleanly under independent verification. Two factual nits: opening month is September 2012 not October, and the site hours quote is slightly garbled in the claim. The visibility-pattern intuition — strong on Michelin/James Beard/design-district queries, weak on Coachella-Valley-wide rollups — matches what I see in live SERPs. Yelp review count (1,596) is verbatim correct; other platform counts are plausible but not independently sampled.
